summary: Strong Loose Lucy & Visions, solid Alabama jam, tight drums
blurb: The show delivers a standout Loose Lucy and an inspired Visions Of Johanna, while the second set builds with China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider and a powerful Alabama Getaway jam. Jerry’s guitar stays on point though his voice shows fatigue late in the night. The recording captures tight, punchy drums and a clear mix, making it a solid 1995 performance.
review: On March 30, 1995 at The Omni in Atlanta, the Dead put together a memorable evening that balances classic energy with some wear‑and‑tear on Jerry’s vocals. Set one opens strong with Touch of Grey and Good Morning Little School Girl before launching into a fiery Loose Lucy that many fans cite as a highlight. Alabama Getaway stretches out with an extended jam that keeps the momentum rolling into the second set, where China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider ignites the crowd and leads into a soulful Looks Like Rain. The centerpiece is a soaring Visions Of Johanna that showcases the band’s tight interplay, followed by Throwing Stones and a rousing Turn On Your Lovelight. The encore closes on a psychedelic high with Lucy In The Sky With Diamonds. Throughout, Mickey and Bill deliver a tougher, punchy drum sound that grounds the performance, while Vince Welnick provides steady keys support. Though Jerry’s voice sounds a bit tired toward the end, his guitar work remains solid and the overall flow stays engaging. The Miller soundboard capture (gd1995-03-30.sbd.miller.119786.flac16) offers excellent clarity, balanced mix, and no major dropouts, making it the recommended listening source for this show.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings note strong performances (Loose Lucy, Visions Of Johanna, Alabama Getaway) and praise the tight drum mix. One source is fully positive while the other is mixed, mentioning some vocal fatigue but no consistent negative criticism across songs. The overall consensus leans positive, warranting a moderate sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with clear, balanced mix and a tight, punchy drum sound; no major dropouts.

key_highlights: Loose Lucy delivers an energetic showcase early in the set.
Visions Of Johanna shines as the second‑set centerpiece.
Extended Alabama Getaway jam provides a powerful peak.
China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider sequence drives momentum.
song_highlights: Lucy In The Sky With Diamonds

band_performance.Jerry: Solid guitar work throughout; vocals show some fatigue toward the end, especially during the drum break.
band_performance.Mickey: Tougher, punchy drum sound praised; drummers interact well with Jerry during outros.
band_performance.Billy: Tight, energetic playing that anchors the extended jams.

must_listen_sequences: Lucy In The Sky With Diamonds
